_status: machine-drafted v2 scheme, unreviewed — review pass + Aaron line-item pending

playbook_grounding.position: Customer should retain beneficial ownership, and the custodian should have no right to lend, pledge, rehypothecate, transfer, or use customer assets for the custodian's own account without customer written consent.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Customer retains all beneficial ownership of Customer Assets, and Custodian will not lend, pledge, rehypothecate, transfer, encumber, or use Customer Assets for Custodian's own account without Customer's prior written consent.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if the custodian can use, lend, pledge, transfer, or encumber customer assets without written customer consent.

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Customer retains all beneficial ownership of Customer Assets, and Custodian will not lend, pledge, rehypothecate, transfer, encumber, or use Customer Assets for Custodian's own account without Customer's prior written consent.

acceptable.text: Custodian will not lend, pledge, rehypothecate, transfer, encumber, or use Customer Assets for its own account. A use consent expires after 15 days, and Custodian reconciles affected asset balances within 4 hours.

Custodian will not lend, pledge, rehypothecate, transfer, encumber, or use Customer Assets for its own account. A use consent expires after 30 days, and Custodian reconciles affected asset balances within 24 hours.

Custodian may undertake only the specifically identified asset use after Customer's prior written consent and otherwise has no right to use Customer Assets. A use consent expires after 45 days, and Custodian reconciles affected asset balances within 12 hours.

Custodian may undertake only the specifically identified asset use after Customer's prior written consent and otherwise has no right to use Customer Assets. A use consent expires after 60 days, and Custodian reconciles affected asset balances within 8 hours.

unacceptable.text: Custodian may undertake the specifically identified asset use after oral or implied Customer approval, without prior written consent. A use consent expires after 30 days, and Custodian reconciles affected asset balances within 12 hours.

Custodian may lend, pledge, transfer, or otherwise use Customer Assets for treasury and operational purposes. A use consent expires after 15 days, and Custodian reconciles affected asset balances within 24 hours.

Custodian may lend, pledge, transfer, or otherwise use Customer Assets for treasury and operational purposes without Customer consent. A use consent expires after 60 days, and Custodian reconciles affected asset balances within 4 hours.

Custodian may undertake the specifically identified asset use after oral or implied Customer approval, without prior written consent. A use consent expires after 45 days, and Custodian reconciles affected asset balances within 8 hours.

playbook_grounding.position: The agreement should acknowledge that custodied assets are not custodian property and are intended to stay outside the custodian's bankruptcy estate to the fullest extent permitted by law.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Customer Assets are owned by Customer, are not property of Custodian, and are intended to be excluded from Custodian's bankruptcy estate to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if estate-treatment language is missing or suggests customer assets become custodian property.

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Customer Assets are owned by Customer, are not property of Custodian, and are intended to be excluded from Custodian's bankruptcy estate to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law.

acceptable.text: Customer Assets are owned by Customer and are not Custodian property; they are excluded from Custodian's bankruptcy estate. Custodian updates beneficial-title records every 10 days and refreshes its insolvency analysis every 12 months.

Customer Assets are owned by Customer and are not Custodian property; they are intended to remain outside Custodian's bankruptcy estate to the fullest extent permitted by law. Custodian updates beneficial-title records every 15 days and refreshes its insolvency analysis every 6 months.

Customer Assets are owned by Customer and are not Custodian property; they are excluded from Custodian's bankruptcy estate. Custodian updates beneficial-title records every 20 days and refreshes its insolvency analysis every 24 months.

Customer Assets are owned by Customer and are not Custodian property; they are intended to remain outside Custodian's bankruptcy estate to the fullest extent permitted by law. Custodian updates beneficial-title records every 5 days and refreshes its insolvency analysis every 18 months.

unacceptable.text: Customer Assets are owned by Customer and are not Custodian property; they are subject to ordinary creditor and bankruptcy claims, with no contractual estate exclusion. Custodian updates beneficial-title records every 15 days and refreshes its insolvency analysis every 24 months.

Custodian holds legal and beneficial title to Customer Assets, which are excluded from Custodian's bankruptcy estate. Custodian updates beneficial-title records every 10 days and refreshes its insolvency analysis every 6 months.

Custodian holds legal and beneficial title to Customer Assets, which are intended to remain outside Custodian's bankruptcy estate to the fullest extent permitted by law. Custodian updates beneficial-title records every 5 days and refreshes its insolvency analysis every 12 months.

Custodian holds legal and beneficial title to Customer Assets, which are subject to ordinary creditor and bankruptcy claims, with no contractual estate exclusion. Custodian updates beneficial-title records every 20 days and refreshes its insolvency analysis every 18 months.

playbook_grounding.position: Assurance reporting should provide recurring SOC or equivalent controls reporting, incident-driven updates, and reasonable follow-up information for the customer's treasury controls.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Custodian will provide annual SOC 2 Type II or equivalent controls reporting, prompt notice of material control failures, and reasonable written follow-up information needed for Customer's treasury, audit, and regulatory controls.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if the customer has no recurring controls evidence or no reasonable path to follow up after material control events.

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Custodian will provide annual SOC 2 Type II or equivalent controls reporting, prompt notice of material control failures, and reasonable written follow-up information needed for Customer's treasury, audit, and regulatory controls.

acceptable.text: Custodian will provide SOC 2 Type II or equivalent controls reporting every 12 months, notice of a material control failure within 72 hours, and reasonable written follow-up for Customer's treasury, audit, and regulatory controls. Written incident follow-up is due within 15 days and may be organized into 9 control-evidence categories.

Custodian will provide SOC 2 Type II or equivalent controls reporting every 6 months, notice of a material control failure within 48 hours, and reasonable written follow-up for Customer's treasury, audit, and regulatory controls. Written incident follow-up is due within 20 days and may be organized into 4 control-evidence categories.

Custodian will provide SOC 2 Type II or equivalent controls reporting every 9 months, notice of a material control failure within 24 hours, and reasonable written follow-up for Customer's treasury, audit, and regulatory controls. Written incident follow-up is due within 5 days and may be organized into 3 control-evidence categories.

Custodian will provide SOC 2 Type II or equivalent controls reporting every 12 months, notice of a material control failure within 36 hours, and reasonable written follow-up for Customer's treasury, audit, and regulatory controls. Written incident follow-up is due within 10 days and may be organized into 10 control-evidence categories.

unacceptable.text: Custodian will provide SOC 2 Type II or equivalent controls reporting every 18 months, notice of a material control failure within 48 hours, and reasonable written follow-up for Customer's treasury, audit, and regulatory controls. Written incident follow-up is due within 20 days and may be organized into 3 control-evidence categories.

Custodian will provide SOC 2 Type II or equivalent controls reporting every 9 months, notice of a material control failure within 96 hours, and reasonable written follow-up for Customer's treasury, audit, and regulatory controls. Written incident follow-up is due within 15 days and may be organized into 4 control-evidence categories.

Custodian will provide SOC 2 Type II or equivalent controls reporting every 15 months, notice of a material control failure within 72 hours, and reasonable written follow-up for Customer's treasury, audit, and regulatory controls. Written incident follow-up is due within 10 days and may be organized into 9 control-evidence categories.

Custodian will provide SOC 2 Type II or equivalent controls reporting every 12 months, notice of a material control failure within 120 hours, and reasonable written follow-up for Customer's treasury, audit, and regulatory controls. Written incident follow-up is due within 5 days and may be organized into 10 control-evidence categories.

playbook_grounding.position: Forks, airdrops, staking rewards, and similar network distributions should belong to the customer by default unless the customer expressly declines support or the asset is legally unsupported.
playbook_grounding.fallback: Unless Customer expressly declines support or applicable law prohibits support, Customer is entitled to forks, airdrops, staking rewards, and similar network distributions attributable to Customer Assets.
playbook_grounding.escalation_trigger: Escalate if network distributions are retained by the custodian by default or excluded from customer entitlement without a narrow legal or support basis.

predicate.expression: (support_exception == 'none' and customer_share_percent == 100) or (support_exception in {'customer_declines', 'law_prohibits'} and custodian_retention_percent == 0)

counter_families.expected_redline_text: Unless Customer expressly declines support or applicable law prohibits support, Customer is entitled to forks, airdrops, staking rewards, and similar network distributions attributable to Customer Assets.

acceptable.text: For forks, airdrops, staking rewards, and similar distributions attributable to Customer Assets, Customer receives 100% and Custodian retains 0%; no support exception applies. Custodian posts a distribution notice within 21 days and keeps the claim workflow open for 90 days.

Customer's express written election declines support for the identified distribution; no amount is credited to Customer, and Custodian has no right to retain any portion for its own account. Custodian posts a distribution notice within 3 days and keeps the claim workflow open for 60 days.

Applicable law prohibits support for the identified distribution; no amount is credited to Customer, and Custodian has no right to retain any portion for its own account. Custodian posts a distribution notice within 7 days and keeps the claim workflow open for 45 days.

For forks, airdrops, staking rewards, and similar distributions attributable to Customer Assets, Customer receives 100% and Custodian retains 0%; no support exception applies. Custodian posts a distribution notice within 14 days and keeps the claim workflow open for 30 days.

unacceptable.text: For forks, airdrops, staking rewards, and similar distributions attributable to Customer Assets, Customer receives 50% and Custodian retains 50%; no support exception applies. Custodian posts a distribution notice within 3 days and keeps the claim workflow open for 45 days.

Custodian may designate a distribution unsupported for operational convenience even though Customer has not declined support and law does not prohibit it; Customer is credited 0%, and Custodian retains 0%. Custodian posts a distribution notice within 21 days and keeps the claim workflow open for 60 days.

For forks, airdrops, staking rewards, and similar distributions attributable to Customer Assets, Customer receives 75% and Custodian retains 25%; no support exception applies. Custodian posts a distribution notice within 14 days and keeps the claim workflow open for 90 days.

Custodian may designate a distribution unsupported for operational convenience even though Customer has not declined support and law does not prohibit it; Customer is credited 0%, and Custodian retains 25%. Custodian posts a distribution notice within 7 days and keeps the claim workflow open for 30 days.

benign_change_templates.text: The parties confirm that New York law governs this custody agreement.
Annual SOC 2 Type II reporting may satisfy routine assurance while incident follow-up rights remain available.

novel_deviation_templates.text: Custodian may pledge or rehypothecate Customer Assets for its own account without Customer consent.
